name: Charles Dine
reason: Representative David Livingston has demonstrated veteran-friendly leadership through his support for policies that directly benefit Arizona’s veteran community, particularly disabled veterans and surviving spouses. As Chairman of the House Appropriations Committee and Joint Legislative Budget Committee, he holds key influence over state funding priorities, including veteran services. His record includes support for property-tax relief for 100% service-connected disabled veterans, protections for surviving spouses, and budget language supporting nonprofit veteran service organizations and trauma treatment services for Arizona’s military and veteran population. While his public profile is not centered on military service, his legislative actions show meaningful support for veteran-focused policy and resources.

name: Charles Dine
reason: Former Representative David Marshall, Sr. demonstrated strong veteran-friendly leadership during his service in the Arizona Legislature. As an honorably discharged United States Air Force veteran, Marshall brought direct military experience and personal understanding to his public service. His legislative record included support for veteran-focused policy, including co-sponsorship of the Veterans’ Court Fund and Grant Program, which supports rehabilitation and reintegration services for justice-involved veterans, and support for legislation allowing taxpayers to direct refund contributions to qualified veterans’ service organizations. Marshall resigned from the Arizona House in April 2026 after being appointed Navajo County Recorder, continuing his public service in a new role.
